One day soon Speaker Pelosi may require anyone working in the United States Congress to be vaccinated or face yet-to-be announced consequences. After that, it’s anyone’s guess as to who else will be required to get a shot. With what we know about how Nancy Pelosi operates; it wouldn’t be surprising if she is already doing her part to implement a country-wide vaccine mandate.

Democrats told us that the pandemic would be “defeated” when enough people were inoculated to reach herd immunity. The thing is, they keep moving the bar on us.

How can we trust a government run by folks who said that if you got vaccinated, you could take your mask off? **Then soon after, back to masks, no matter your vaccine status!**

The simple answer is this: we can’t trust Democrats in DC to stick to their word.

**Democrats enact mask mandates. Then don’t wear them.**

Democrats restrict social gatherings. Then break their own mandates to host oversized gatherings where virtually no one is masked.

**Democrats want to require covid vaccine passports but let COVID positive illegal immigrants cross the southern border.**

_Will the hypocrisy ever end?_

name: Bruno Amato
subject: Yesterday's email
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bruno Amato for Congress
body:
| | |
---
Folks, **A month ago** , Kevin McCarthy was one of 16 Republicans who voted AGAINST the ALLIES Act which is legislation that increased the number of visas available for Afghan interpreters who had worked with U.S. personnel, as the Taliban advanced in their control. **During the past few days** , Kevin McCarthy has been using every chance he gets to rail against the Biden Administration’s handling of Afghanistan, commenting on the ongoing humanitarian crisis and trying to score political points all while doing it. **Yesterday** , I sent an email asking you to step up and take action in supporting Afghans as the crisis has mounted with the Taliban control and thousands of Afghans, allies and U.S. personnel are attempting to leave Afghanistan. [In case you missed my email and you’re looking for ways to help, check out this resource to contribute funds and other necessary needs with the Lutheran Immigration and Refugee Service](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/nIATlT34azO8Uq6tATWtxGptS1CHuft_cQWgiB76hN4rYSvzh8DwivSGYT05mFpTBdR1M2JEY_5n7RPPHSkCMv52dT-uB9E08ilcUzo4NIsOK_6Hq0elGAkDuOKJjENEzPO7jYL3AwWqd092ZdU71vpyutH2s4wovatTI7FmunpU55X_S3pvRgjOvjZI_UBEhG2yfb3C31BHFP-lJ4KeSru7gRf5NtnvvAy0BIee3n2kNryrXQg1QGw5_aKvtNOU5fQVfOGl7dp8kaUCjTPHPQ/3ej/q7mt4wF1QLCWzG7WuBPsCg/h0/aN5PvI0D7__h34czotQRbICF-Tg1Fk7JmnvcTqzJNKU). Here’s the thing, folks. I should not have to send those types of emails. Don’t get me wrong, it is incredibly important to lend our support and contribute wherever we can in the face of such enormous crises. **But, folks, why is it on us to step up and do what we can — with the limited resources and power that we as regular people have — when members of Congress could have passed legislation**** _yesterday_****to expand funding and support for welcoming the increasing number of asylum seekers?** Folks, the job of a member of Congress should be to step up in these moments — they should lead and they should reach beyond what may seem possible in order to help the people they serve. That is the world we are trying to build. That is what I will do as a member of Congress. Folks, I’m running for Congress _because we can no longer wait_ on Kevin McCarthy to lead when he has refused to do so at every turn. This is a grassroots movement and every dollar counts in our efforts to defeat him. [Will you chip in whatever you can today so we can build this movement and reimagine what it means to lead?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xk9NwEyjAoWg-sb9VIPpLoOJ93kwfgcC_Eur18075pTMAVv7rAbBsLeNi0CmBSuWnXEvldu0u6HRuLdINZDWdOaF4fw5cbMqMw-pvjrM23gKOySQgjVqvbO4biLIYzCNqV5Jj_g3AD-nTlVbX3yS-CBpkh-ZA2Hj6Q-nuJlprEhtqQMJPjkCGIDBau1Dqpm58QLT0cscVLegWAvk0cymZuHVLpXVLLF-q4h_Vam-RY-Q2cOIKlq-rPlqaVExSYb-axI5EHaQjfaA8yM2flX7mY/3ej/q7mt4wF1QLCWzG7WuBPsCg/h1/hP8TYFVvGePx6xAPv7onJ7T4D5GftIGsMBqRad-p4ok) Thank you, folks. I’m so grateful to be building this movement with you. In solidarity, Bruno Amato

clean_body: | | | Folks, A month ago , Kevin McCarthy was one of 16 Republicans who voted AGAINST the ALLIES Act which is legislation that increased the number of visas available for Afghan interpreters who had worked with U.S. personnel, as the Taliban advanced in their control. During the past few days , Kevin McCarthy has been using every chance he gets to rail against the Biden Administration’s handling of Afghanistan, commenting on the ongoing humanitarian crisis and trying to score political points all while doing it. Yesterday , I sent an email asking you to step up and take action in supporting Afghans as the crisis has mounted with the Taliban control and thousands of Afghans, allies and U.S. personnel are attempting to leave Afghanistan. . Here’s the thing, folks. I should not have to send those types of emails. Don’t get me wrong, it is incredibly important to lend our support and contribute wherever we can in the face of such enormous crises. But, folks, why is it on us to step up and do what we can — with the limited resources and power that we as regular people have — when members of Congress could have passed legislation yesterdayto expand funding and support for welcoming the increasing number of asylum seekers? Folks, the job of a member of Congress should be to step up in these moments — they should lead and they should reach beyond what may seem possible in order to help the people they serve. That is the world we are trying to build. That is what I will do as a member of Congress. Folks, I’m running for Congress because we can no longer wait on Kevin McCarthy to lead when he has refused to do so at every turn. This is a grassroots movement and every dollar counts in our efforts to defeat him. Thank you, folks. I’m so grateful to be building this movement with you. In solidarity, Bruno Amato * | | ---|--- Paid for by Bruno Amato for Congress

body:
| | | I'm so sorry to bother you on your Tuesday evening -- but I had to reach out one last time before my midnight deadline.

My team just crunched the numbers, and it appears we're still falling short of my House Democrats' must-hit Mid Month goal.

And since you're one of my very best grassroots supporters, **[I'm hoping you could chip in to protect House Democrats and help close the gap before time runs out in just SIX hours.](https://links.e.dccc.org/els/v2/AGmXQyYv~)**

I won't sugarcoat it:**Failure is just simply not an option here.**

Earlier this month, Republicans shattered their FIFTH fundraising record in a row -- and now have $7 million more in the bank than my House Democrats.

**And if we don 't turn the tide soon, I fear we could lose our House Majority and everything we've worked for.**

**[So please, if you could chip in $3, $](https://links.e.dccc.org/els/v2/x4-)10[, $](https://links.e.dccc.org/els/v2/x4-)25[ \-- or anything you can spare -- before midnight, I'd be so grateful.](https://links.e.dccc.org/els/v2/x4-)**

| _If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Thank you,

Nancy

name: Team AOC
subject: How the Child Tax Credit is helping families
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Read how the child tax credit is helping working families across the country.
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20210817-2350-header&t=1&akid=2350%2E2526796%2E2v07k3)
In March, Congress expanded the Child Tax Credit as part of President Biden’s American Rescue Plan to provide monthly payments of up to $300 per child.
The day after the first payments went out in July, we sent around a survey to ask those who received a payment how it would help their family. We received hundreds of responses.
**Here are a few answers we received to the question:_How will your expanded child tax credit payment help you and your family?_**
“Before Covid, my wife and I were able to arrange our schedules so that someone was always home with our daughter. My wife lost her job, and the new one is not as flexible as the old one – so our kid is in daycare for the first time. We had no room in our budget for daycare costs, (especially because my wife's less flexible new job also pays less than the one that laid her off), but the monthly tax credit money is making it possible.” - Brittney
“It's given us the room to be able to fix our car. We've needed new brakes for a while and now our car can be safe to drive.” - Shaye
“It helps others so it helps me.” - William
“Buy a new crib and diapers.” - Eric
“It’s going to help with groceries, school supplies and clothes. It will also help with vehicle payments and electric bills! I am VERY THANKFUL!!” - Hilarie
“It helps us a lot paying our rent and other household expenses. As a person earning minimum wage, this helps my family breathe easier during these hard times.” - John
“Before the pandemic, I had a high yield savings account for my baby boy. After losing my job, I had no choice but to delve into that account to keep us afloat. Now, I will be able to put back what I spent by the end of the year PLUS catch up on bills while getting him what he needs as he enters the toddler stage. I am very grateful for the extra help.” - Jacqueline
“This extended child tax credit payment is an acknowledgment of how difficult it has been for decades of working mothers and families to just get by.” - Diane
“It makes me SO HAPPY. But I'm not getting it myself – my children are grown. Still so great.” - Lynn
We know the child tax credit is working, and not just because we read hundreds of stories like these from across the country.
**New data from the U.S. Census Bureau shows that hunger rates dropped nearly 24% as a result of the payments.** 1 Nearly half – 47 percent – of households reported spending their tax credit payment on food.
Alexandria and her progressive colleagues fought tooth and nail to bring as many benefits to working people as possible in the American Rescue Plan (you can watch AOC break down everything in the plan [**here**](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/9390?t=2&akid=2350%2E2526796%2E2v07k3)).
We will continue fighting to bring more direct relief for working families – but we need your help. [If you can afford it, please make a $3 contribution today to help us continue fighting for the relief working families need.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20210817?refcode=em20210817-2350-P&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&refcode2=2350_2526796_2v07k3&akid=2350%2E2526796%2E2v07k3)
Thank you for being with us in the fight.
– Team AOC

clean_body: Read how the child tax credit is helping working families across the country. In March, Congress expanded the Child Tax Credit as part of President Biden’s American Rescue Plan to provide monthly payments of up to $300 per child. The day after the first payments went out in July, we sent around a survey to ask those who received a payment how it would help their family. We received hundreds of responses. Here are a few answers we received to the question:How will your expanded child tax credit payment help you and your family? “Before Covid, my wife and I were able to arrange our schedules so that someone was always home with our daughter. My wife lost her job, and the new one is not as flexible as the old one – so our kid is in daycare for the first time. We had no room in our budget for daycare costs, (especially because my wife's less flexible new job also pays less than the one that laid her off), but the monthly tax credit money is making it possible.” - Brittney “It's given us the room to be able to fix our car. We've needed new brakes for a while and now our car can be safe to drive.” - Shaye “It helps others so it helps me.” - William “Buy a new crib and diapers.” - Eric “It’s going to help with groceries, school supplies and clothes. It will also help with vehicle payments and electric bills! I am VERY THANKFUL!!” - Hilarie “It helps us a lot paying our rent and other household expenses. As a person earning minimum wage, this helps my family breathe easier during these hard times.” - John “Before the pandemic, I had a high yield savings account for my baby boy. After losing my job, I had no choice but to delve into that account to keep us afloat. Now, I will be able to put back what I spent by the end of the year PLUS catch up on bills while getting him what he needs as he enters the toddler stage. I am very grateful for the extra help.” - Jacqueline “This extended child tax credit payment is an acknowledgment of how difficult it has been for decades of working mothers and families to just get by.” - Diane “It makes me SO HAPPY. But I'm not getting it myself – my children are grown. Still so great.” - Lynn We know the child tax credit is working, and not just because we read hundreds of stories like these from across the country. New data from the U.S. Census Bureau shows that hunger rates dropped nearly 24% as a result of the payments. 1 Nearly half – 47 percent – of households reported spending their tax credit payment on food. Alexandria and her progressive colleagues fought tooth and nail to bring as many benefits to working people as possible in the American Rescue Plan (you can watch AOC break down everything in the plan ). We will continue fighting to bring more direct relief for working families – but we need your help. Thank you for being with us in the fight. – Team AOC P.S.
